question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing quarterly restaurant performance indicators, what can we conclude about which quarter has the best overall performance?

options.A: Q3 shows the best overall performance because it has the highest combined bar height.
options.B: Q1 shows the best overall performance because it has the lowest number of customer complaints.
options.C: Q4 shows the best overall performance because it has the highest sales alone.
options.D: We cannot reliably determine the best overall performance from this chart because it stacks together indicators with different units that cannot be meaningfully added.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
